description: Well-known identifiers for tensionsDeclared[].tension. Producers SHOULD use one of these IDs when the named tension applies. For domain-specific tensions not in this registry, use the 'x:' prefix per SPECIFICATION.md §5.1.11.

entries.name: CAP / PACELC
entries.summary: Strong consistency, high availability, and low latency cannot coexist. During a partition, consistency and availability are a binary fork. Even without partitions, consistency and latency are coupled — synchronous replication adds real milliseconds.

entries.name: Observability cost trilemma
entries.summary: Full-resolution observability at full scale costs more than the application infrastructure. Resolution, cost, and real-time freshness form a three-body problem — you must sample, delay, or pay.
entries.cite: Industry folklore; surfaced by Honeycomb, Grafana, Datadog operational writeups.

entries.name: Test suite coverage vs. maintainability
entries.summary: High-coverage tests tightly coupled to implementation create a codebase that cannot evolve. 95% coverage with brittle tests is worse than 70% coverage with behavioral tests.
entries.cite: Fowler test pyramid; Beck test-first principles.

entries.name: Diagnosability vs. PII exposure
entries.summary: Rich structured logs with context-specific fields improve diagnosability but can leak PII into log aggregators with weaker access controls than the primary database.
entries.cite: GDPR Article 5(1)(c) data minimization; OWASP Logging Cheat Sheet.

entries.name: Security boundary enforcement vs. latency
entries.summary: Sandboxing, input validation, mTLS, and least-privilege enforcement cost real compute. Latency targets must account for security boundary overhead.
